responses.summary: Reporting documented sustained, large-volume groundwater withdrawals at Meta's Newton County campus and questioned whether the local utility's disclosure framework was adequate to surface the impact to nearby residents.
responses.source_title: Grist — Meta data center groundwater coverage (Newton County, GA)

responses.summary: The Louisiana Public Service Commission opened a docket reviewing Entergy's proposed gas-fired generation buildout to serve Meta's Richland Parish load, with intervenor groups raising concerns about cost allocation to existing ratepayers.
responses.source_title: Louisiana Public Service Commission — Entergy IRP filings

responses.summary: Crook County officials have repeatedly cited Meta's Prineville campus as a major contributor to local property tax revenue and as the largest private employer in the county.
responses.source_title: Crook County — Economic development statements

responses.summary: The Oregonian successfully sued the City of The Dalles to obtain Google's water-use figures, which the city had argued were trade secrets; the released numbers showed Google's facility consuming roughly a quarter of the city's water.
responses.source_title: The Oregonian — Google water-use disclosure lawsuit (2022)

responses.summary: The Council Bluffs and Pottawattamie County governments have publicly highlighted Google's investment as transformative for local infrastructure spending, citing road, fiber, and school funding tied to the campus.
responses.source_title: City of Council Bluffs — Google partnership statements

responses.summary: Arizona water-policy advocates criticized the siting of large data centers — including Microsoft's Goodyear campus — in the metro Phoenix area amid ongoing groundwater scarcity and limits on new residential development.
responses.source_title: Arizona Department of Water Resources — Phoenix AMA assured-water findings

responses.summary: Wisconsin state and Racine County officials welcomed Microsoft's expanded $3.3B commitment as repurposing the long-stalled Foxconn site, with paired pledges of community investment and workforce training programs.
responses.source_title: Racine County — Microsoft Mount Pleasant announcement

responses.summary: Loudoun County residents living near data center clusters raised noise and visual-impact concerns at multiple Board of Supervisors hearings; the county subsequently tightened zoning and decibel rules for new builds.
responses.source_title: Loudoun County Board of Supervisors — Data center zoning hearings (2024)

responses.summary: FERC declined to approve an amended interconnection agreement that would have allowed AWS's Cumberland Valley campus to draw additional behind-the-meter capacity from the Susquehanna nuclear plant, citing potential cost-shifting to other PJM ratepayers.
responses.source_title: FERC — Talen / AWS / Susquehanna ISA filing decision (Nov 2024)

responses.summary: The NAACP and the Southern Environmental Law Center notified xAI of intent to sue over Clean Air Act violations tied to dozens of unpermitted methane gas turbines installed at the Colossus site in a majority-Black south Memphis neighborhood.
responses.source_title: SELC — Notice of intent to sue xAI over Memphis air permits

responses.summary: South Memphis residents told reporters and the Shelby County Health Department that emissions and noise from xAI's on-site gas turbines worsened existing air-quality concerns in a neighborhood already burdened by industrial pollution.
responses.source_title: Memphis Commercial Appeal — Boxtown residents on xAI air quality

responses.summary: Abilene and Taylor County officials publicly welcomed the Stargate buildout, citing expected construction employment and the deepening of West Texas as an AI-infrastructure hub.
responses.source_title: City of Abilene — Stargate project statements

responses.summary: Utah Governor Spencer Cox defended the project's strategic importance in the U.S.–China AI race but acknowledged residents' concerns and announced the project would be scaled back from the original 40,000 acres and ~7.5 GW to roughly 2,000 acres and 1.5 GW.
responses.source_title: Beyond data centers: Utah governor issues wake-up call on new Cold War contest over AI — Deseret News

responses.summary: The Box Elder County Commission voted to approve a development consent agreement for the Wonder Valley campus, citing expected jobs, economic development, and the project's national-security framing.
responses.source_title: Hundreds cry out as Box Elder commissioners wave in massive data center — Utah News Dispatch

responses.summary: The Sierra Club's Utah Chapter, led by director Franque Bains, condemned the Box Elder County Commission's approval as prioritizing polluters over residents and warned that the project's water and energy demand would worsen the Great Salt Lake water crisis.
responses.source_title: Stratos Data Center Approval Threatens Great Salt Lake Basin — Sierra Club

responses.summary: Utah Clean Energy estimated the proposed 9 GW gas-powered campus would consume nearly double Utah's 2025 peak electricity demand, increase statewide CO2 emissions by 55–75%, and use between 2 and 16.6 billion gallons of water per year depending on the gas-generation technology selected.
responses.source_title: Estimated Emissions and Water Consumption from the Proposed Stratos Data Center — Utah Clean Energy

responses.summary: Elevate Utah's policy brief argues the Stratos project rests on a 92% energy tax cut and an 80% property-tax rebate to the developer, would roughly double Utah's power demand on natural gas, and was approved with no completed environmental review or public vote on the Great Salt Lake watershed.
responses.source_title: The Little* Data Center on the Prairie — Elevate Utah

responses.summary: Brigham City resident Brenna Williams, lead sponsor of the Box Elder Accountability Referendum (BEAR), filed two referendum applications and used the group's public organizing platform to argue that local residents — not an out-of-state billionaire — should decide whether the 9 GW campus moves forward.
responses.source_title: Referendums filed to challenge Stratos-MIDA Data Center in Box Elder County — KUTV

responses.summary: Tremonton resident Farrah Pliley, who runs the social-media channels for the Box Elder Accountability Referendum, organized residents into a video and volunteer-recruiting campaign aimed at qualifying a county-wide vote to overturn the Stratos approval.
responses.source_title: Box Elder community group seeks to have voices heard in data center project — Fox 13 Now

responses.summary: Utah Residents Organized for Oversight, Transparency and Stewardship (Utah ROOTS), led publicly by Aubri Devashrayee, characterized opposition to the Stratos campus as a cross-partisan coalition objecting to the speed and opacity of the MIDA approval process.
responses.source_title: Box Elder County data center debate intensifies as foes, supporters trade barbs — KSL

responses.summary: Alliant Energy president and CEO Lisa Barton publicly committed to a five-year, zero-percent rate freeze for existing Iowa residential, farm, and small-business customers, framing the QTS load as additive rather than rate-base-shifted, and citing affordability protection as the company's rationale for backing the QTS Ratepayer Protection Pledge.
responses.source_title: U.S. Energy Secretary tours Cedar Rapids QTS data center campus — The Gazette

responses.summary: Iowa energy and consumer advocates told The Daily Iowan that the demand spike from QTS and other Cedar Rapids data centers could ultimately raise residential utility bills if Alliant Energy passes infrastructure-build costs through to households despite QTS's pledge to fund its own energy needs. Advocates are watching to see whether the pledge translates into binding tariff structures at the Iowa Utilities Commission.
responses.source_title: Cedar Rapids data centers ignite concern from energy advocates, excitement from officials — The Daily Iowan

responses.summary: Cedar Rapids Mayor Tiffany O'Donnell publicly endorsed the QTS project during the U.S. Energy Secretary's site tour, citing land-stewardship safeguards and downstream interest from companies wanting proximity to the data-center campus.
responses.source_title: U.S. Energy Secretary tours QTS data center, addresses utility, water concerns — KCRG

responses.summary: New Market Village residents in Henrico County's Varina district have raised concerns about water consumption, noise, power demand, environmental degradation, and what they describe as opaque deal-making around data-center development including QTS facilities. Henrico supervisors responded by tightening data-center oversight rules.
responses.source_title: DATA CENTER DETOUR: Inside Henrico County's shifting mindset — Henrico Citizen

responses.summary: The Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council designated QTS Richmond Technology Park Data Center 5 as the first-ever data-center project to receive FAST-41 coverage, naming the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers as federal lead. The Council framed the designation as implementing the President's Executive Order to streamline federal review for AI infrastructure.
responses.source_title: First Data Center Project Gains Permitting Council's FAST-41 Coverage — permitting.gov

responses.summary: The American Battlefield Trust and the Oak Valley Homeowners Association sued Prince William County over the rezoning approval for QTS's ~2,139-acre Prince William Digital Gateway, arguing the project encroaches on Manassas-area battlefield land and rural residential character. The Virginia Court of Appeals ruled in plaintiffs' favor; QTS filed an 11th-hour appeal.
responses.source_title: QTS files 11th hour appeal to keep the Prince William Digital Gateway alive — Prince William Times

responses.summary: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel issued a guarded welcome of Google's commitment to a contested case before the Michigan Public Service Commission, suggesting Google had 'learned a lesson' from the prior expedited Oracle/OpenAI Saline Township process by accepting independent expert and public scrutiny rather than seeking ex parte approval.
responses.source_title: AG Nessel on Newly Announced Google Data Center Proposal

responses.summary: Over 1,500 residents have signed a petition opposing the Van Buren Township data center, citing concerns about water consumption (estimated 2-3.6 million gallons daily), grid strain on existing DTE customers, and proximity to residential neighborhoods west of Detroit Wayne International Airport.
responses.source_title: Google, DTE plan 1 gigawatt Michigan data center, eye Van Buren Township site

responses.summary: Cheyenne Mayor Patrick Collins welcomed Microsoft's expansion as a continuation of a 14-year partnership, noting Microsoft is the city's largest taxpayer and framing the build-out as an opportunity to retain Wyoming high-school graduates who otherwise leave the state at among the highest rates in the country.
responses.source_title: Microsoft To Triple Its Cheyenne Data Center Footprint With 3,200-Acre Land Buy

responses.summary: Wyoming State Senator Cale Case (R-Lander) raised concerns about grid congestion and the practical limits of statutory protections for residential ratepayers, cautioning that interconnection-driven infrastructure costs can spill over to existing customers regardless of nominal tariff rules.
responses.source_title: Microsoft To Triple Its Cheyenne Data Center Footprint With 3,200-Acre Land Buy

responses.summary: Ohio Governor Mike DeWine intervened earlier in 2026 to preserve a state sales-tax exemption for data centers and characterized rising electricity prices as 'a legitimate discussion,' while expressing confidence that local opposition would not constrain the industry's growth in Ohio.
responses.source_title: Lordstown AI data center, manufacturing hub, likely to boost jobs and demand energy

responses.summary: Prineville's public works director and Crook County commissioner publicly endorsed Meta's $1M-$3.2M funding of a second aquifer storage and recovery injection well, projected to double the city's annual storage capacity to 300 million gallons. An Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife biologist noted long-term groundwater development will eventually reduce surface flows.
responses.source_title: The Bulletin — Prineville doubles down on water conservation with tech giant-backed project

responses.summary: A Good Jobs First study found Crook County School District's foregone revenue from data-center property tax abatements rose from $10M in 2019 to $29M in 2024, even as Meta has donated more than $2M to the district over a decade. The district's CFO acknowledged the trade-off but said enrollment has grown with new data-center jobs.
responses.source_title: The Bulletin — Oregon business property tax breaks cost schools $275 million last year

responses.summary: A Central Oregon angling and watershed-advocacy blog argued Meta's data-center groundwater pumping has dropped Facebook's Prineville well by 53 feet from 2010 to 2020 and that mitigation water released in winter does not address summer low-flow conditions on the Crooked River.
responses.source_title: Central Oregon Informed Angler — Prineville, Data Centers, and Water: There is a Cost

responses.summary: The Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton and Walton counties unanimously approved disbursing a $3 million PILOT payment from Meta, with Newton County's 37.5% share ($1.125M) directed to schools, the county commission and fire services. The disbursement was part of an FY23 record-high $10.5M JDA return.
responses.source_title: The Covington News — Newton County to receive $1.125 million from Meta PILOT payment

responses.summary: Beverly and Jeff Morris, plus at least three neighboring households roughly 1,000 feet from Meta's Stanton Springs site, reported well failures, sediment, and discolored water beginning during data-center construction. Newton County Water and Sewerage Authority director Mike Hopkins said the area's community water budget cannot accommodate further data-center demand.
responses.source_title: ppc.land — Meta data center impacts local water supply in Newton County

responses.summary: Mansfield Mayor Blair Northen cited a 33% local water-rate increase over two years — well above the typical ~2% annual adjustment — and Chattahoochee Riverkeeper documented that subsequent data-center applicants have requested up to 9 million gallons per day, raising questions about whether the existing tariff and disclosure framework can keep up with demand.
responses.source_title: ppc.land — Meta data center impacts local water supply in Newton County

responses.summary: The Louisiana Public Service Commission approved Entergy's plan to build three gas plants (2,262 MW) and a $550M transmission line to serve Meta's Richland Parish load. Commissioner Davante Lewis cast the lone dissenting vote, citing nondisclosure agreements that prevent the public from seeing how much Meta is paying versus what existing ratepayers will bear.
responses.source_title: Fox 8 — Louisiana's $3B power upgrade for Meta project raises questions about who should foot the bill

responses.summary: Sierra Club's reporting characterized the Richland Parish project as straining Louisiana's grid (twice New Orleans peak demand), adding three new gas plants likely to worsen regional air quality, and using ~5 million gallons of water daily — concerns echoed by an Alliance for Affordable Energy advocate that ratepayers will be left exposed to long-run costs Meta has not contractually committed to cover.
responses.source_title: Sierra — In Rural Louisiana, Meta's New Data Center Promises Growth — But at What Cost?

responses.summary: Richland Parish Tax Assessor Emmett 'Lee' Brown described rents jumping from roughly $600-$700/month to $2,500/month with the influx of about 4,000 construction-related residents, and longtime resident Joyce Piercy described traffic and affordability strain even as local businesses report record sales. Officials project >$2B in property tax revenue over the project's life.
responses.source_title: Fox 8 — Meta's $27 billion AI data center is transforming rural Louisiana

responses.summary: The Northeast Federation of Teachers & School Employees said Richland Parish's sales-tax-funded annual teacher 'clean-out check' is set to grow sharply — by union president Sandie Lollie's account, sales-tax collections supporting the bonus have grown nearly 2,000% — driven by Meta-related construction spending across Monroe-area restaurants, hotels and services.
responses.source_title: KNOE — Richland Parish teachers could see bigger end-of-year checks

responses.summary: The Sierra Club's Virginia Chapter released a 2025 report calling unrestrained data center growth 'unsustainable,' citing 1 billion gallons of water consumed by Loudoun County data centers in 2023 and arguing Amazon's renewable-energy investments fall significantly short of actual power consumption.
responses.source_title: Sierra Club's Virginia chapter says data center growth is unsustainable

responses.summary: On March 18, 2025, the Loudoun County Board of Supervisors eliminated 'by-right' data center zoning in Industrial Park, General Industry, and Mineral Resources-Heavy Industry districts, requiring all new projects to undergo Special Exception review and public input.
responses.source_title: Loudoun County, Virginia, Eliminates By-Right Data Center Development

responses.summary: The Loudoun Climate Project, part of the Virginia Data Center Reform Coalition, advocated for the 2025 zoning rollback and is pushing 'Five Pillars of Data Center Reform' for 2026 state legislation, citing power grid strain, transmission line conflicts in residential areas, and ratepayer cost-shifting.
responses.source_title: The Dark Side of Data Centers — Loudoun Climate Project

responses.summary: The Indiana Department of Environmental Management cited Amazon and contractor Walbridge Construction for unauthorized wetland impacts at the New Carlisle site and ordered work halted on affected portions; an April 30 IDEM public hearing drew over 100 speakers opposed to a permit covering ~10 acres of wetland and 5,000 feet of stream.
responses.source_title: Amazon data center in New Carlisle cited by IDEM for impacting wetlands without authorization

responses.summary: Residents west of South Bend reported residential wells running dry as Amazon and a neighboring GM/Samsung battery plant ramped construction; the Indiana Department of Natural Resources confirmed it was investigating six well failures while not attributing cause.
responses.source_title: Residents raise concerns after wells lose water near Amazon, GM projects in St. Joseph County

responses.summary: The Hoosier Environmental Council formally opposed Amazon's wetland-fill permit, calling proposed off-site mitigation insufficient and citing failures of Indiana's wetland mitigation credit program; the group argued the wetlands a mile from Bendix Woods Nature Preserve should be preserved on site.
responses.source_title: Wetland loss and data center clash at IDEM public hearing — Hoosier Environmental Council

responses.summary: The South Bend Regional Chamber of Commerce launched a dedicated webpage promoting Project Rainier, framing it as transformational economic development and rebutting common community concerns about water and power.
responses.source_title: Chamber launches webpage to promote Amazon data center

responses.summary: FERC denied Talen Energy's rehearing request and upheld its November 2024 rejection of the amended interconnection service agreement that would have boosted AWS behind-the-meter draw from 300 MW to 480 MW, prompting Talen to file an appeal in the Fifth Circuit.
responses.source_title: FERC upholds rejection of proposed interconnection agreement

responses.summary: Exelon and American Electric Power filed a joint protest at FERC against the AWS-Talen interconnection agreement, warning it could allow the data center to avoid up to $140 million per year in transmission costs and shift them to other PJM ratepayers.
responses.source_title: Exelon and AEP protest interconnection agreement for Amazon data center at Susquehanna nuclear plant

responses.summary: FERC unanimously directed PJM to establish new colocation rules for data centers at power plants, creating three transmission service options designed to address the cost-shifting concerns raised in the AWS-Talen Susquehanna case while permitting structured behind-the-meter arrangements going forward.
responses.source_title: FERC Directs Nation's Largest Grid Operator to Create New Rules to Embrace Innovation and Protect Consumers
